lines.text: Mimika, as you requested, I ordered a fully equipped team to get ready at our branch.
Though I still don't understand why you had all the Explosive Spears moved to the branch...
What's on your mind exactly, Mimika? It should be time you tell us.
I-I didn't expect that!
Won't this trigger immediate explosions upon landing?
Hey, Mimika. The catapult is all set.
Hey Rover, remember when you met the Inferno Rider and it whispered “Rush… Till the end...”?
Maybe inside that TD, the wishes of the fallen Midnight Rangers still linger.
Rover, if you can outrun that TD, those fallen solders may help you… Help you reach the end, the finish line…
Derived from the fighting spirit of past warriors, the Inferno Rider, in its Echo form, delivered a powerful final blow to the Tacet Mark core of those Incinero Petals...
Perhaps it's just me, but... I think the fallen Midnight Rangers would be happy to see this.
Then please come visit our branch when you're back in Jinzhou. We'll be waiting for you.
Like it or not, we've already declined their package. Let's just pretend this order never existed.
Even if I had, do you seriously think we could've delivered it?
So why did you even make the promise in the first place? Ugh...
You're talking about the Sea of Flames in Guixu Port City. There's no way I'm letting any of our staff go to such a dangerous place!
Besides, who would order anything to a place like that? It's obviously a prank. What was even in the package?
Even if that's the case, and I'm not saying it is... We wouldn't make much, just barely enough to cover our costs.
Listen, if they really needed it, wouldn't they just place another order?
Enough! This is my call to make as manager. Leave it.
Why you...!
Wait... This is the rejection email I sent out! How did it end up here...?
Mimika! You must have sent it to the wrong recipient when you grabbed my touchpad earlier!
Oh my... I can’t stand this...
I'm really sorry we wasted your time because of our mistake...
Mimika! Come here and apologize to the customer.
Thank you so much for understanding. You are one of those rare, generous individuals.
I-idle, is it? Hmm, what a luxury... Ahem! Hehe, that is a relief.
Anyway, it was entirely our fault that caused all this...
We'll issue a formal apology later, but for now, please allow me to sort out this strange order.
Do you not know about Sea of Flames, {Male=sir;Female=miss}?
Your knowledge is truly impressive! In that case, I needn't blabber on about how dangerous that place is.
Hm. The flower there is spectacular for sure, but it's lethal at the same time.
You really don't know? The Sea of Flames is a vast field of Incinero Petals whose pollen is extremely toxic.
Not to mention the horrible TDs! Few have made it out there alive, as far as I know.
Now you see how dangerous it is, Mimika?
I said no!
Just take no for an answer... Ugh, she's so stubborn. {Male=Sir;Female=Miss}, could you help me convince her?
You heard it? Those are words of reason! Don't go there!
{Male=Sir;Female=Miss}! What are you...
Hey!!!
Damn it! What now... She must have left for the Sea of Flames!
Why? Of all places, why there...
Sigh... We don't let our employees go near the Sea of Flames. That place is off-limits.
The incident dates back to when Lollo Logistics established our first Jinzhou branch.
That place got overrun by Incinero Petals, spewing toxic pollen that drove everyone away.
And to make things worse, the Center for Wutherological Climate Studies found those Gravity Storms raging in the area. Talk about adding insult to injury.
If that pollen ever got swept up into the upper atmosphere by those storms, it would spell disaster for Jinzhou and the surrounding regions.
Absolutely. At the time, the researchers figured out that the only way to stop the spread was to deactivate the Tacet Mark core of those flowers.
The Huaxu Academy created Explosive Spears to do the job, but the terrain was too dangerous for any regular vehicle to get through.
Most of the Tacetite vehicles were tied up at the frontlines with the Midnight Rangers, fighting off the TD Outbreak, so we couldn't get the Spears to the center of the Sea of Flames.

That left Lollo Logistics to step in with our spare Tacetite vehicles and deliver them. The plan was for the Midnight Rangers to use them to take out the Tacet Marks, risking their lives in the process.
Everyone knew how dangerous the job was, but we took it on anyway—after all, our motto is "We Promise, We Deliver."
But... we couldn't get there in time. Our couriers tried their best, but they couldn't deliver the Spears in time.
In the end, only one Spear made it through, and even though we fell short, the Midnight Rangers thanked us for our help.
They had most of their people escort our couriers to safety, while a few brave souls stayed behind to charge at the Tacet Mark, knowing it could cost them their lives.
They passed the Spear along, one after another, trying to get it to the Tacet Mark. But the deadly pollen was too much. In the end, they all ended up Overclocking.
Sigh... The Spear got damaged during transport, so it didn't fully deactivate the Tacet Mark when it hit. The explosion never went off.
But, at least the attack weakened the Tacet Mark enough to stop the spread of the Incinero Petals, and the pollen concentration dropped to a level that no longer threatens Jinzhou.
Even though no one blamed them, the survivors at Lollo Logistics were wracked with guilt, thinking their mistake cost those brave soldiers their lives.
That mission left a permanent scar on our company. After that, we made it a rule that any major order had to be handled by Resonators with combat skills.
"We Promise, We Deliver"... It's just easier said than done...
Now you see why that place is such a taboo for us. We don't want couriers like Mimika getting mixed up with the Sea of Flames.
All right. That's enough blabbering from me. Let’s focus on finding Mimika...
Ah, that girl is as stubborn as a mule and never listens to anyone, but she's also known for being ridiculously fast. If I don't head out right now, I might not be able to catch up to her.
No, no, {Male=sir;Female=miss}! How could I bother you with this...
Eh... But...
All right then. Thank you very much for your assistance.
Let's search for her on the outskirts of the Sea of Flames first. I've sent you the coordinates.
Would you go with me, {Male=sir;Female=miss}?
Okay. Then I'll go first. I'll see you there.
Thank goodness you both made it out safe this time. If anything had happened, you'd have not only gotten yourself killed, but also one of our customers!
I swear, if anything had happened to you two, I'd never forgive myself.
Oh, you're awake!
Let me explain. While she was tracking down the person who placed the order, the touchpad she grabbed got swept away by the Gravity Storm.
She got lost and wandered around until she bumped into me. I managed to get her out of there.
Yeah, it must've been blown there by the Gravity Storm.
The Incinero Petals weren't too bad, but I didn’t expect such a powerful Tacet Discord to be lurking there...
Ugh... I wasn't always a manager, you know. I used to be a top courier, and getting people out of danger was just part of the job.
Hey, watch it...
I dread remembering it, but that TD... I think it was saying... "Charge... till the end..."
Back then, the Midnight Rangers who attacked the Tacet Mark core ended up creating a Tacet Field when they Overclocked. That Tacet Discord might've come from that.
I was one of the couriers who delivered the Explosive Spears. I saw it all happen.
The determination of those soldiers has now become the biggest obstacle to destroying the Tacet Mark. It's tragic.
What are you talking about...
What are you rambling about? No way.
Stop. Lollo Logistics can't get involved anymore. We need to stick to our business principles.
Even if I agreed, headquarters would never approve. It's not profitable and it risks our employees' safety.
Ugh... I said no, and I meant it!
Huh? What did you say?
If even someone as strong as {PlayerName} can't handle it... Mimika, you should probably give up.
Wh-what?!
A hundred thousand Credits... That's a pretty substantial order.
Hmm...
Alright! We at Lollo Logistics will take the order. We'll do everything we can to support you!
I heard you, I heard you! No need to shout. My ears are ringing from the noise.
Please excuse us, {Male=sir;Female=miss}. Mimika is always so noisy. She must have caused a lot of trouble for you back in the Port City of Guixu.
As I anticipated... I'm so, so sorry, {Male=sir;Female=miss}!
Anyway, I do need to thank you, Mimika.
Your insistence saved that customer in the Sea of Flames and ensured successful delivery of the Spears. Without you, our customer may have been consumed by TDs.
You're right, Mimika. "We Promise, We Deliver"... This should never be a mere slogan. It should be the faith we stick to.
That being said, I must, however, ask you to reflect on your impulsiveness.
That's the spirit. You will become an excellent courier one day.
Finally, on behalf of Lollo Logistics, allow me to express our sincerest gratitude to you, dear customer.
Without your help, this crisis would not have been resolved so well.
We would probably have to doubt ourselves in regret and shame. It would be a disgrace to our slogan, "We Promise, We Deliver!"
Therefore, we thank you... for letting us honor our promise and deliver this overdue order.
The one-hundred-thousand-credit order you placed with us is hereby finished, but since it's your first order, a discount must apply.
The discounted amount will be returned to you as a token of our appreciation, mine especially. Please accept it.
